title: Handling Error -262 After Migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or Later
breadcrumbs: Handling Error -262 After Migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or Later
html:
Handling Error -262 After Migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or Later

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication
Watch
Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedApr 02, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

This section provides guideline to handle the procerror -262 (UWEBERR_DSP_PROTOCOL) that might appear after migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or later. It is intended for Uniface web applications that previously sent non-DSP HTTP requests to DSP components and now fail due to stricter DSP request verification introduced since Uniface 10.4.02.

Description

Since Uniface 10.4.02, USYSHTTP performs stricter validation of DSP requests. HTTP requests that do not meet these enhanced verification rules fail earlier, during request interpretation in USYSHTTP, with error -262.

This behavior improves the security of Uniface web applications and helps prevent errors from occurring later during execution inside user-developed DSP components.

This issue is most commonly encountered when an application submits a plain HTML form directly to a DSP component without using DSP binding for the corresponding fields.

A Typical Use Case

A common failing pattern is when:

  • A DSP page contains a plain HTML form in the layout.
  • The form fields are not bound to DSP fields.
  • The submit button is a standard HTML submit button.
  • The form posts directly to a DSP URL.

This is a standard HTML form with fields in the HTML layout, unlike a typical DSP layout with DSP-bound fields in it.

When a user clicks on the submit button, an HTTP request is sent to userver. Before 10.4.02, this request was handled by USYSHTTP without error; but since 10.4.02, this request fails with the following errors:

Example:
<form action="http://target.domain:8080/uniface/wrd/DSP1" method="post">
  <input name="email" value="uniface_user@example.com">
  <input name="name" value="Uniface User">
  <input type="submit" value="Submit">
</form>

This is a standard HTML form with fields in the HTML layout, unlike a typical DSP layout with DSP-bound fields in it.

When a user clicks on the submit button, an HTTP request is sent to userver. Before 10.4.02, this request was handled by USYSHTTP without error; but since 10.4.02, this request fails with the following errors:
  • Error number: -262
  • Error constant: UWEBERR_DSP_PROTOCOL
  • Description: An error occurred while interpreting a DSP request
  • Additional reason: Verification failed for DSP request

Why This Happens

The behavior changed in Uniface 10.4.02 because USYSHTTP was re-engineered and the DSP request validation became stricter.

Prior to version 10.4.02, USYSHTTP processed HTTP requests targeting DSP components in nearly the same way as requests targeting USP and SVC components. As a result, requests such as standard HTML form submissions were allowed to pass through and handled by the target DSP without additional validation.

From version 10.4.02 onward, USYSHTTP processes HTTP requests based on the target component type. Requests targeting DSP components are now handled by a dedicated DSP request handler, which performs stricter verification of incoming requests.

This handler expects requests to originate from the DSP front-end JavaScript framework. Specifically, a DSP-targeting request must satisfy at least one of the following conditions:
  • The request is sent through interaction with a widget bound to a DSP field.
  • The request is sent by invoking a DSP operation via the DSP JavaScript API.

Requests that do not meet these criteria, that is, requests that do not originate from the DSP front end, may fail validation in USYSHTTP and result in the error -262.

Hence, if an existing application posts a regular HTML form directly to a DSP, that request is rejected during DSP request verification in Uniface 10.4.02 and later.

Resolution to fix the error -262 after migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or Later

We suggest the following options:

  • Option 1: if applicable, consider to migrate the target DSP from an HTML form layout to a DSP layout bound with entity;
  • Option 2: Consider to change the target component from DSP to SVC or USP.
  • Option 3: If the application need a centralized fix, consider to customize USYSHTTP.

Option 1: Migrate to a real DSP page

Instead of a HTML form submit, use a real DSP page as follows:

Assume the target DSP has an entity ENTITY1.MODEL1 (if not, create one), add the HTML form field to this entity. For example:
  • Field EMAIL with widget EditBox;
  • Field NAME with widget EditBox;
  • Field SUBMIT with widget CommandButton and initial value Submit.
In the DSP layout, instead of a HTML form, add the DSP-bound entity and field as following code:
<table id="uent:ENTITY1.MODEL1" border="1">
 <tbody id="uocc:ENTITY.NOMODEL">
  <tr>
   <td>
    <input id="ufld:EMAIL.ENTITY.NOMODEL" value="Input Element"/>
    <input id="ufld:NAME.ENTITY.NOMODEL" value="Input Element"/>  
    <!--
      more input field here ...
    -->

   </td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
   <td>
    <input id="ufld:SUBMIT.ENTITY.NOMODEL" value="Input Element"/>
   </td>
  <tr>
 </tbody>
</table>

Option 2: Change the target component from DSP to SVC or USP

Instead of migrating to a DSP, an alternative way is using SVC or USP as the target component as follows:

  • Create a new SVC or USP with the target operation (exec, or others) copied from your original target DSP component;

  • Change your form action URL from a DSP to this new target SVC or USP.

Option 3: Customizing USYSHTTP

Includes:
  • Error -262 handling;
  • DSP white list, security consideration

Error handling

Sample code
Users need to import and update the USYSHTTP component. Implement in USYSHTTP component in entity DSPhandler.
partner operation getRequest
throws
 try
  call usyshttp::dspGetRequest()
 catch -262
  ; DSP request verification failed

  ; Optional, for better security, do a white list check for particular components
  call whiteListCheck()

  ; Bend the call to be a full page static call
  $webinfo("requesttype") = "STATIC"
  putitem/id $webinfo("WEBSERVERCONTEXT"), "ACTIVATE", "DSPbasic"
  putitem/id $webinfo("WEBSERVERCONTEXT"), "REQUESTTYPE", "static"
  putitem/id $webinfo("WEBSERVERCONTEXT"), "EXPECTEDRESPONSE", "fullpage"
  
 endtry
end
 
entry whiteListCheck
throws
variables
  string vCompName, vOperationName
endvariables
  vCompName = $uppercase($item("COMP", $webinfo("WEBSERVERCONTEXT")))
  vOperationName = $item("OPERATION", $webinfo("WEBSERVERCONTEXT"))
  selectcase $uppercase(vCompName)
    case "DSP1"
      if (vOperationName = "exec")
        return 
      endif
      ;more cases
      ;case "DSP2"
    elsecase
      throw <UWEBERR_DSP_PROTOCOL>, "Your error message.", "REASON=Your reason"
  endselectcase
end
Related Links
  • USYSHTTP

    The USYSHTTP component is a global web application request processor that handles all web requests sent to a Uniface Server via the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD).

  • $procerror

    Return the reason for an error in ProcScript execution.

Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
